26	// executeCapturingStub is a FizeauService that records Execute calls and fails
27	// loudly if ResolveRoute is invoked — enforcing the CONTRACT-003 boundary
28	// that execution paths must not pre-resolve routes.
29	type executeCapturingStub struct {
30		mu            sync.Mutex
31		executeCalled bool
32		lastReq       agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
33		executionReq  agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
34		executionSeen bool
35		executeFn     func(agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error)
36		listModels    []agentlib.ModelInfo
37		listProfiles  []agentlib.ProfileInfo
38	}
39	
40	func (s *executeCapturingStub) Execute(_ context.Context, req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
41		s.mu.Lock()
42		s.executeCalled = true
43		s.lastReq = req
44		if req.Role == "implementer" {
45			s.executionReq = req
46			s.executionSeen = true
47		}
48		s.mu.Unlock()
49		if s.executeFn != nil {
50			return s.executeFn(req)
51		}
52		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
53		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
54		close(ch)
55		return ch, nil
56	}
57	
58	func (s *executeCapturingStub) ResolveRoute(_ context.Context, _ agentlib.RouteRequest) (*agentlib.RouteDecision, error) {
59		return nil, fmt.Errorf("routinglint: ResolveRoute called in execution path — violates CONTRACT-003 / ddx-da19756a")
60	}

107	// installExecuteCapturingStub injects stub as the service used by
108	// RunWithConfigViaService and registers cleanup. Returns the stub so callers
109	// can inspect captured fields after the run.
110	func installExecuteCapturingStub(t *testing.T) *executeCapturingStub {
111		t.Helper()
112		stub := &executeCapturingStub{}
113		agent.SetServiceRunFactory(func(_ string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error) {
114			return stub, nil
115		})
116		t.Cleanup(func() { agent.SetServiceRunFactory(nil) })
117		return stub
118	}

144	// TestRunPassthroughHarnessModelToExecute (AC): ddx agent run --harness claude
145	// --model opus forwards those constraints to ServiceExecuteRequest without
146	// calling ResolveRoute.
147	func TestRunPassthroughHarnessModelToExecute(t *testing.T) {
148		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
149	
150		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
151	
152		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
153		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
154		_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "run",
155			"--harness", "claude",
156			"--model", "claude-opus-4-7",
157			"--text", "hello",
158			"--timeout", "5s",
159		)
160		// Stub Execute returns success, so the command should succeed.
161		// If ResolveRoute were called, Execute returns an error from the stub.
162		require.NoError(t, err, "run must succeed; if ResolveRoute was called the stub returns an error")
163	
164		stub.mu.Lock()
165		executeCalled := stub.executeCalled
166		lastReq := stub.lastReq
167		stub.mu.Unlock()
168	
169		require.True(t, executeCalled, "Execute must be called on the service stub")
170		assert.Equal(t, "claude", lastReq.Harness, "Harness must pass through unchanged to Execute")
171		assert.Equal(t, "claude-opus-4-7", lastReq.Model, "Model must pass through unchanged to Execute")
172	}
173	
174	// TestRunPassthroughEmptyConstraintsToExecute (AC): ddx agent run with no
175	// harness/model flags calls Execute with empty passthrough — the upstream
176	// service selects routing without DDx pre-resolution.
177	func TestRunPassthroughEmptyConstraintsToExecute(t *testing.T) {
178		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
179	
180		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
181	
182		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
183		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
184		_, _ = executeCommand(root, "agent", "run", "--text", "hello", "--timeout", "5s")
185		// Command may fail (no real provider configured) but Execute must be called.
186	
187		stub.mu.Lock()
188		executeCalled := stub.executeCalled
189		lastReq := stub.lastReq
190		stub.mu.Unlock()
191	
192		if !executeCalled {
193			t.Skip("Execute not called — agent may have been short-circuited before service dispatch")
194		}
195		assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Harness, "Harness must be empty when no --harness flag provided")
196		assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Model, "Model must be empty when no --model flag provided")
197	}

199	// TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute (AC): ddx work must not call ResolveRoute
200	// in the execution path. The stub returns an error from ResolveRoute so any
201	// call to it would surface as a command failure carrying "ResolveRoute called".
202	// MinPower/MaxPower passthrough to ServiceExecuteRequest is proved statically
203	// by the singleTierAttempt loopOverrides construction and by the internal
204	// agent package's passthrough_envelope_test.go.
205	func TestWorkDoesNotCallResolveRoute(t *testing.T) {
206		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
207	
208		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
209		_ = stub // stub.ResolveRoute returns error; any call would propagate up
210	
211		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
212	
213		// Seed one ready bead so the loop has something to claim.
214		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
215		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
216		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
217			ID:    "ddx-passthrough-power-test",
218			Title: "passthrough power test bead",
219		}))
220	
221		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
222		_, err := executeCommand(root, "work",
223			"--once",
224			"--harness", "claude",
225			"--model", "claude-opus-4-7",
226			"--min-power", "40",
227			"--max-power", "90",
228		)
229		// If ResolveRoute were called, the stub returns an error containing
230		// "ResolveRoute called in execution path". Verify it didn't happen.
231		if err != nil {
232			require.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "ResolveRoute called in execution path",
233				"ddx work must not call ResolveRoute (CONTRACT-003 / ddx-da19756a)")
234		}
235		// Other errors (git not initialized, etc.) are expected and acceptable —
236		// the test's only concern is that ResolveRoute was not invoked.
237	}

313	func TestDDxWork_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook(t *testing.T) {
314		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
315	
316		var mu sync.Mutex
317		modes := make([]string, 0, 4)
318		var intakeReq agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest
319		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
320		stub.listProfiles = []agentlib.ProfileInfo{
321			{Name: "cheap", MinPower: 5, MaxPower: 5},
322			{Name: "smart", MinPower: 9, MaxPower: 10},
323		}
324		stub.listModels = []agentlib.ModelInfo{
325			{ID: "cheap-model", Power: 5, Available: true, AutoRoutable: true},
326			{ID: "smart-model", Power: 9, Available: true, AutoRoutable: true},
327		}
328		stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
329			mode := "execute"
330			switch {
331			case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake"):
332				mode = "intake"
333			case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: lint"):
334				mode = "lint"
335			case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: triage"):
336				mode = "triage"
337			}
338			mu.Lock()
339			modes = append(modes, mode)
340			if mode == "intake" {
341				intakeReq = req
342			}
343			mu.Unlock()
344	
345			switch mode {
346			case "intake":
347				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
348				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"atomic\",\"confidence\":0.99,\"reasoning\":\"single-slice\"}"}`)}
349				close(ch)
350				return ch, nil
351			case "lint":
352				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
353				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"score\":9,\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[]}"}`)}
354				close(ch)
355				return ch, nil
356			case "triage":
357				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
358				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"already_satisfied\",\"recommended_action\":\"close_already_satisfied\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
359				close(ch)
360				return ch, nil
361			default:
362				ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
363				ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
364				close(ch)
365				return ch, nil
366			}
367		}
368	
369		dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
370		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
371		_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--max-power", "8")
372	
373		mu.Lock()
374		got := append([]string(nil), modes...)
375		gotIntakeReq := intakeReq
376		mu.Unlock()
377		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 1, "work must invoke the intake hook")
378		assert.Equal(t, "intake", got[0], "plain ddx work must run pre-claim intake before claim")
379		assert.Equal(t, "smart", gotIntakeReq.Profile)
380		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.Harness)
381		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.Provider)
382		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.Model)
383		assert.Empty(t, gotIntakeReq.ModelRef)
384		assert.Zero(t, gotIntakeReq.MinPower)
385		assert.Zero(t, gotIntakeReq.MaxPower, "intake dispatch must not inherit implementation power bounds")
386		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(got), 2, "work must continue past intake to later execution stages")
387	}

389	// TestExecuteBeadDoesNotCallResolveRoute (AC4 / ddx-6036e1bc): ddx agent
390	// execute-bead must not call ResolveRoute in the execution path. The stub
391	// installed by installExecuteCapturingStub returns an error from ResolveRoute,
392	// so any call to it would surface as a command failure containing
393	// "ResolveRoute called in execution path".
394	//
395	// Static guarantee: TestRoutinglintNonStatusFilesDoNotCallResolveRoute already
396	// ensures agent_execute_bead.go has no direct .ResolveRoute() call. This
397	// behavioral test adds a runtime check at the cmd layer.
398	func TestExecuteBeadDoesNotCallResolveRoute(t *testing.T) {
399		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
400	
401		stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
402		_ = stub // stub.ResolveRoute returns error; any call would propagate up
403	
404		dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
405	
406		// Seed a bead so execute-bead has something to load before reaching git.
407		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
408		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
409		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
410			ID:    "ddx-no-resolve-route-test",
411			Title: "execute-bead no ResolveRoute test",
412		}))
413	
414		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
415		_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead",
416			"ddx-no-resolve-route-test",
417			"--harness", "agent",
418		)
419		// execute-bead may fail (no git repo), but must not call ResolveRoute.
420		if err != nil {
421			require.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "ResolveRoute called in execution path",
422				"execute-bead must not call ResolveRoute (CONTRACT-003 / ddx-6036e1bc)")
423		}
424	}

21	type workerIntakeServiceStub struct {

23		modes      []string

28	func (s *workerIntakeServiceStub) Execute(_ context.Context, req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
29		mode := "execute"
30		switch {
31		case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake"):
32			mode = "intake"
33		case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: lint"):
34			mode = "lint"
35		case strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: triage"):
36			mode = "triage"
37		}
38		s.mu.Lock()
39		s.modes = append(s.modes, mode)
40		s.mu.Unlock()
41	
42		if s.executeErr != nil && mode == "execute" {
43			return nil, s.executeErr
44		}
45	
46		finalText := `ok`
47		switch mode {
48		case "intake":
49			finalText = `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"single-slice"}`
50		case "lint":
51			finalText = `{"score":9,"rationale":"ok","suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}`
52		case "triage":
53			finalText = `{"classification":"already_satisfied","recommended_action":"close_already_satisfied","rationale":"ok","suggested_amendments":[],"suggested_followup_beads":[]}`
54		}
55	
56		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
57		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":` + fmt.Sprintf("%q", finalText) + `}`)}
58		close(ch)
59		return ch, nil
60	}

1	package cmd

3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"strings"
7		"testing"
8	
9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
11	)
12	
13	// TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified covers ddx-b790449b AC1
14	// and AC2: a project with .ddx/beads.jsonl but no .ddx/config.yaml must drain
15	// against the global agent config defaults instead of failing with the
16	// upstream "routing under-specified" error. The test sets HOME to a temp
17	// directory holding a minimal agent config and runs `ddx work --once`
18	// programmatically. It asserts:
19	//   - the command does not return "routing under-specified" (the regression
20	//     this hot-fix addresses)
21	//   - no .ddx/config.yaml is created as a side effect of the run
22	//
23	// Real provider dispatch is not exercised: the bead's actual execution may
24	// fail because the temp config points at an unreachable endpoint, but that
25	// failure is downstream of the routing decision and unrelated to AC1.
26	func TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified(t *testing.T) {
27		projectDir := t.TempDir()
28		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".ddx")
29		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
30	
31		// Single trivial open bead so the loop has something to route.
32		beads := `{"id":"test-zero-cfg-1","title":"trivial","type":"task","status":"open","priority":3,"created_at":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z"}` + "\n"
33		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "beads.jsonl"), []byte(beads), 0o644))
34	
35		// Synthesize a HOME with a minimal global agent config so the
36		// providersHasProviders precheck passes. The provider points at a
37		// localhost port no test will ever bind — actual dispatch will fail
38		// but the routing-under-specified gate must not.
39		homeDir := t.TempDir()
40		agentCfgDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config", "agent")
41		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentCfgDir, 0o755))
42		agentCfg := `providers:
43	  testprov:
44	    type: lmstudio
45	    base_url: http://127.0.0.1:1
46	    api_key: test
47	    model: test-model
48	default_provider: testprov
49	`
50		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentCfgDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(agentCfg), 0o644))
51	
52		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
53		t.Setenv("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config"))
54	
55		factory := NewCommandFactory(projectDir)
56		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
57		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--project", projectDir)
58	
59		combined := out
60		if err != nil {
61			combined = combined + " | err=" + err.Error()
62		}
63		// AC1/AC2: the upstream "routing under-specified" message must not surface
64		// for a config-less project when the global config has at least one
65		// provider. If it does, the zero-config fall-through is broken.
66		assert.NotContainsf(t, strings.ToLower(combined), "routing under-specified",
67			"zero-config drain must not emit routing-under-specified; got: %s", combined)
68	
69		// AC1: ddx work must not write .ddx/config.yaml as a side effect.
70		_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"))
71		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
72			"zero-config drain must not create .ddx/config.yaml (statErr=%v)", statErr)
73	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	)
10	
11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
91		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
92		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
93			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
94		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
95		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98	
99		// Register "ddx work plan" and "ddx work focus" as subcommands of "ddx work".
100		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
102	
103		return cmd
104	}
105	
106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	const (
9		ReadinessClassificationReady            = "ready"
10		ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine      = "needs_refine"
11		ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit       = "needs_split"
12		ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired = "operator_required"
13		ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready    = "system_unready"
14	
15		ReadinessReasonTooLarge                       = "too_large"
16		ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope                 = "ambiguous_scope"
17		ReadinessReasonMissingRootCauseOrCurrentState = "missing_root_cause_or_current_state"
18		ReadinessReasonMissingVerification            = "missing_verification"
19		ReadinessReasonMissingCodePathAssertion       = "missing_code_path_assertion"
20		ReadinessReasonMissingDependencyOrParent      = "missing_dependency_or_parent"
21		ReadinessReasonHiddenExternalBlocker          = "hidden_external_blocker"
22		ReadinessReasonAlreadySatisfiedCandidate      = "already_satisfied_candidate"
23		ReadinessReasonSystemUnready                  = "system_unready"
24	
25		ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable       = "unavailable"
26		ReadinessSystemReasonRouting           = "routing"
27		ReadinessSystemReasonQuota             = "quota"
28		ReadinessSystemReasonTransport         = "transport"
29		ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted = "resource_exhausted"
30		ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency   = "repo_concurrency"
31	)
32	
33	// ReadinessClassificationResult is the deterministic bridge between the
34	// bead-lifecycle readiness vocabulary and execute-loop scheduling semantics.
35	type ReadinessClassificationResult struct {
36		Classification       string
37		Reason               string
38		SystemReason         string
39		TriageClassification string
40		IntakeOutcome        PreClaimIntakeOutcome
41	}
42	
43	// ClassifyReadiness maps bead-lifecycle readiness evidence to a stable result.
44	// Infrastructure signals always win over bead-quality signals so routing,
45	// quota, transport, resource, and repository-concurrency failures do not park a
46	// bead as needs_refine/needs_split.
47	func ClassifyReadiness(classification string, reasons []string, detail string) ReadinessClassificationResult {
48		classification = normalizeReadinessClassification(classification)
49		reason := firstReadinessReason(reasons)
50		systemReason := classifyReadinessSystemReason(detail, reasons)
51		if classification == ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready ||
52			reason == ReadinessReasonSystemUnready ||
53			systemReason != "" {
54			if systemReason == "" {
55				systemReason = ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable
56			}
57			return ReadinessClassificationResult{
58				Classification:       ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready,
59				Reason:               ReadinessReasonSystemUnready,
60				SystemReason:         systemReason,
61				TriageClassification: triageClassificationForSystemReason(systemReason),
62				IntakeOutcome:        PreClaimIntakeError,
63			}
64		}
65	
66		if fromReason := readinessClassificationForReason(reason); fromReason != "" {
67			classification = fromReason
68		}
69		if classification == "" {
70			classification = ReadinessClassificationReady
71		}
72	
73		out := ReadinessClassificationResult{
74			Classification: classification,
75			Reason:         reason,
76		}
77		switch classification {
78		case ReadinessClassificationReady:
79			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
80		case ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit:
81			if out.Reason == "" {
82				out.Reason = ReadinessReasonTooLarge
83			}
84			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed
85		case ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired:
86			if out.Reason == "" {
87				out.Reason = ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope
88			}
89			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired
90		case ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine:
91			// Without a validated rewrite, a refinement need is not executable.
92			// The caller may override this to ActionableButRewritten after
93			// validating replacement text from the readiness payload.
94			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired
95		default:
96			out.Classification = ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
97			out.Reason = ReadinessReasonSystemUnready
98			out.SystemReason = ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable
99			out.TriageClassification = triageClassificationForSystemReason(out.SystemReason)
100			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeError
101		}
102		return out
103	}
104	
105	func normalizeReadinessClassification(classification string) string {
106		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(classification)) {
107		case "", "ready", "actionable_atomic", "atomic", "ok", "actionable", "pass":
108			return ReadinessClassificationReady
109		case "needs_refine", "actionable_but_rewritten", "rewritten":
110			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine
111		case "needs_split", "too_large", "too_large_decomposed", "decomposable":
112			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit
113		case "operator_required", "human_review_required":
114			return ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired
115		case "system_unready", "readiness_error", "intake_error":
116			return ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
117		default:
118			return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(classification))
119		}
120	}
121	
122	func normalizeReadinessReason(reason string) string {
123		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(reason)) {
124		case ReadinessReasonTooLarge,
125			ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope,
126			ReadinessReasonMissingRootCauseOrCurrentState,
127			ReadinessReasonMissingVerification,
128			ReadinessReasonMissingCodePathAssertion,
129			ReadinessReasonMissingDependencyOrParent,
130			ReadinessReasonHiddenExternalBlocker,
131			ReadinessReasonAlreadySatisfiedCandidate,
132			ReadinessReasonSystemUnready:
133			return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(reason))
134		default:
135			return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(reason))
136		}
137	}
138	
139	func firstReadinessReason(reasons []string) string {
140		for _, reason := range reasons {
141			reason = normalizeReadinessReason(reason)
142			if reason != "" {
143				return reason
144			}
145		}
146		return ""
147	}
148	
149	func readinessClassificationForReason(reason string) string {
150		switch normalizeReadinessReason(reason) {
151		case ReadinessReasonTooLarge:
152			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit
153		case ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope, ReadinessReasonHiddenExternalBlocker:
154			return ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired
155		case ReadinessReasonMissingRootCauseOrCurrentState,
156			ReadinessReasonMissingVerification,
157			ReadinessReasonMissingCodePathAssertion,
158			ReadinessReasonMissingDependencyOrParent,
159			ReadinessReasonAlreadySatisfiedCandidate:
160			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine
161		case ReadinessReasonSystemUnready:
162			return ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
163		default:
164			return ""
165		}
166	}
167	
168	func classifyReadinessSystemReason(detail string, reasons []string) string {
169		combined := strings.ToLower(strings.Join(append([]string{detail}, reasons...), "\n"))
170		combined = strings.TrimSpace(combined)
171		if combined == "" {
172			return ""
173		}
174		switch {
175		case IsLockContentionError(combined):
176			return ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency
177		case containsAny(combined,
178			"resource_exhausted",
179			"resource exhausted",
180			"enospc",
181			"no space left on device",
182			"out of space",
183			"disk quota exceeded",
184			"temp root is full",
185			"cannot allocate memory"):
186			return ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted
187		case containsAny(combined, "worktree") && containsAny(combined,
188			"failed",
189			"lost",
190			"missing",
191			"cannot",
192			"no such file",
193			"not found",
194			"no space"):
195			return ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted
196		case containsAny(combined, "evidence") && containsAny(combined,
197			"write",
198			"persist",
199			"commit",
200			"bundle") && containsAny(combined,
201			"failed",
202			"no space",
203			"permission denied",
204			"i/o error"):
205			return ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted
206		case containsAny(combined,
207			"429",
208			"too many requests",
209			"rate limit",
210			"rate_limit",
211			"quota",
212			"usage ceiling",
213			"spend cap"):
214			return ReadinessSystemReasonQuota
215		case isTransportError(errors.New(combined)):
216			return ReadinessSystemReasonTransport
217		case containsAny(combined,
218			"resolveroute:",
219			"no viable routing candidate",
220			"no viable provider",
221			"no live provider",
222			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
223			"no harness configured",
224			"missing harness",
225			"harness not installed",
226			"unknown harness",
227			"executable file not found"):
228			return ReadinessSystemReasonRouting
229		default:
230			return ""
231		}
232	}
233	
234	func triageClassificationForSystemReason(systemReason string) string {
235		switch systemReason {
236		case ReadinessSystemReasonRouting:
237			return "routing"
238		case ReadinessSystemReasonQuota:
239			return "quota"
240		case ReadinessSystemReasonTransport:
241			return "transport"
242		case ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
243			ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency,
244			ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable:
245			return "recoverable"
246		default:
247			return ""
248		}
249	}
250	
251	func isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(reason string) bool {
252		switch reason {
253		case "routing",
254			"quota",
255			"transport",
256			"recoverable",
257			ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
258			ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency,
259			FailureModeNoViableProvider,
260			FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled,
261			FailureModeLockContention,
262			FailureModeWorktreeLost:
263			return true
264		default:
265			return false
266		}
267	}

13	func TestReadinessClassification_DoesNotMisclassifyInfrastructureAsBeadDefect(t *testing.T) {
14		cases := []struct {
15			name           string
16			classification string
17			detail         string
18			wantSystem     string
19			wantTriage     string
20		}{
21			{
22				name:       "missing_harness",
23				detail:     "lint hook: missing-harness: no harness configured",
24				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonRouting,
25				wantTriage: "routing",
26			},
27			{
28				name:       "provider_quota",
29				detail:     "provider returned 429 Too Many Requests: quota exceeded",
30				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonQuota,
31				wantTriage: "quota",
32			},
33			{
34				name:       "transport",
35				detail:     "transport failed: connection reset by peer",
36				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonTransport,
37				wantTriage: "transport",
38			},
39			{
40				name:       "enospc",
41				detail:     "resource preflight failed: ENOSPC: no space left on device",
42				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
43				wantTriage: "recoverable",
44			},
45			{
46				name:       "worktree_setup",
47				detail:     "worktree setup failed: no space left on device",
48				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
49				wantTriage: "recoverable",
50			},
51			{
52				name:       "evidence_write",
53				detail:     "evidence bundle write failed: permission denied",
54				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
55				wantTriage: "recoverable",
56			},
57			{
58				name:       "git_index_lock",
59				detail:     "fatal: Unable to create '/repo/.git/index.lock': File exists",
60				wantSystem: ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency,
61				wantTriage: "recoverable",
62			},
63			{
64				name:           "readiness_runner_system_unready",
65				classification: ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready,
66				detail:         "readiness runner exited with empty output",
67				wantSystem:     ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable,
68				wantTriage:     "recoverable",
69			},
70		}
71	
72		for _, tc := range cases {
73			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
74				got := ClassifyReadiness(tc.classification, nil, tc.detail)
75				require.Equal(t, ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready, got.Classification)
76				assert.Equal(t, ReadinessReasonSystemUnready, got.Reason)
77				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantSystem, got.SystemReason)
78				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantTriage, got.TriageClassification)
79				assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeError, got.IntakeOutcome)
80				assert.NotEqual(t, ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine, got.Classification)
81				assert.NotEqual(t, ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit, got.Classification)
82			})
83		}
84	}

86	func TestReadinessClassification_BeadDefectsUseReadinessReasons(t *testing.T) {
87		cases := []struct {
88			name               string
89			reasons            []string
90			wantClassification string
91			wantReason         string
92			wantOutcome        PreClaimIntakeOutcome
93		}{
94			{
95				name:               "too_large",
96				reasons:            []string{ReadinessReasonTooLarge},
97				wantClassification: ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit,
98				wantReason:         ReadinessReasonTooLarge,
99				wantOutcome:        PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
100			},
101			{
102				name:               "ambiguous_scope",
103				reasons:            []string{ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope},
104				wantClassification: ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired,
105				wantReason:         ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope,
106				wantOutcome:        PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired,
107			},
108			{
109				name:               "missing_verification",
110				reasons:            []string{ReadinessReasonMissingVerification},
111				wantClassification: ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine,
112				wantReason:         ReadinessReasonMissingVerification,
113				wantOutcome:        PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired,
114			},
115		}
116	
117		for _, tc := range cases {
118			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
119				got := ClassifyReadiness("", tc.reasons, "bead body failed readiness rubric")
120				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantClassification, got.Classification)
121				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantReason, got.Reason)
122				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantOutcome, got.IntakeOutcome)
123				assert.Empty(t, got.SystemReason)
124				assert.NotEqual(t, ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready, got.Classification)
125			})
126		}
127	}

129	func TestReadinessClassification_DecodesReadinessSchema(t *testing.T) {
130		system, err := decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult(`{"classification":"system_unready","rationale":"ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate"}`)
131		require.NoError(t, err)
132		assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeError, system.Outcome)
133		assert.Equal(t, ReadinessReasonSystemUnready, system.Reason)
134		assert.Equal(t, ReadinessSystemReasonRouting, system.SystemReason)
135	
136		split, err := decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult(`{"classification":"needs_split","rationale":"too broad","readiness_checks":[{"reason":"too_large","verdict":"fail","evidence":"AC spans three subsystems"}]}`)
137		require.NoError(t, err)
138		assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed, split.Outcome)
139		assert.Equal(t, ReadinessReasonTooLarge, split.Reason)
140		assert.Contains(t, split.Detail, ReadinessReasonTooLarge)
141	
142		refine, err := decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult(`{"classification":"needs_refine","rationale":"verification is absent","readiness_checks":[{"reason":"missing_verification","verdict":"fail","evidence":"AC lacks go test"}]}`)
143		require.NoError(t, err)
144		assert.Equal(t, PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, refine.Outcome)
145		assert.Equal(t, ReadinessReasonMissingVerification, refine.Reason)
146		assert.Empty(t, refine.SystemReason)
147	}

149	func TestReadinessClassification_DeterministicSystemReasonBypassesModelTriage(t *testing.T) {
150		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{}
151		report := ExecuteBeadReport{
152			BeadID:        "ddx-system",
153			Status:        ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
154			OutcomeReason: "quota",
155			BaseRev:       "same",
156			ResultRev:     "same",
157		}
158	
159		called := false
160		got := worker.runPostAttemptTriage(context.Background(), bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-system"}, report, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
161			PostAttemptTriageHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error) {
162				called = true
163				return TriageResult{Classification: "needs_investigation", RecommendedAction: "release_claim_needs_investigation"}, nil
164			},
165		}, "worker", time.Now)
166	
167		assert.False(t, called, "deterministic system reasons must not be overwritten by model triage")
168		assert.Equal(t, "quota", got.OutcomeReason)
169	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	const (
9		ReadinessClassificationReady            = "ready"
10		ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine      = "needs_refine"
11		ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit       = "needs_split"
12		ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired = "operator_required"
13		ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready    = "system_unready"
14	
15		ReadinessReasonTooLarge                       = "too_large"
16		ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope                 = "ambiguous_scope"
17		ReadinessReasonMissingRootCauseOrCurrentState = "missing_root_cause_or_current_state"
18		ReadinessReasonMissingVerification            = "missing_verification"
19		ReadinessReasonMissingCodePathAssertion       = "missing_code_path_assertion"
20		ReadinessReasonMissingDependencyOrParent      = "missing_dependency_or_parent"
21		ReadinessReasonHiddenExternalBlocker          = "hidden_external_blocker"
22		ReadinessReasonAlreadySatisfiedCandidate      = "already_satisfied_candidate"
23		ReadinessReasonSystemUnready                  = "system_unready"
24	
25		ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable       = "unavailable"
26		ReadinessSystemReasonRouting           = "routing"
27		ReadinessSystemReasonQuota             = "quota"
28		ReadinessSystemReasonTransport         = "transport"
29		ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted = "resource_exhausted"
30		ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency   = "repo_concurrency"
31	)
32	
33	// ReadinessClassificationResult is the deterministic bridge between the
34	// bead-lifecycle readiness vocabulary and execute-loop scheduling semantics.
35	type ReadinessClassificationResult struct {
36		Classification       string
37		Reason               string
38		SystemReason         string
39		TriageClassification string
40		IntakeOutcome        PreClaimIntakeOutcome
41	}
42	
43	// ClassifyReadiness maps bead-lifecycle readiness evidence to a stable result.
44	// Infrastructure signals always win over bead-quality signals so routing,
45	// quota, transport, resource, and repository-concurrency failures do not park a
46	// bead as needs_refine/needs_split.
47	func ClassifyReadiness(classification string, reasons []string, detail string) ReadinessClassificationResult {
48		classification = normalizeReadinessClassification(classification)
49		reason := firstReadinessReason(reasons)
50		systemReason := classifyReadinessSystemReason(detail, reasons)
51		if classification == ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready ||
52			reason == ReadinessReasonSystemUnready ||
53			systemReason != "" {
54			if systemReason == "" {
55				systemReason = ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable
56			}
57			return ReadinessClassificationResult{
58				Classification:       ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready,
59				Reason:               ReadinessReasonSystemUnready,
60				SystemReason:         systemReason,
61				TriageClassification: triageClassificationForSystemReason(systemReason),
62				IntakeOutcome:        PreClaimIntakeError,
63			}
64		}
65	
66		if fromReason := readinessClassificationForReason(reason); fromReason != "" {
67			classification = fromReason
68		}
69		if classification == "" {
70			classification = ReadinessClassificationReady
71		}
72	
73		out := ReadinessClassificationResult{
74			Classification: classification,
75			Reason:         reason,
76		}
77		switch classification {
78		case ReadinessClassificationReady:
79			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
80		case ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit:
81			if out.Reason == "" {
82				out.Reason = ReadinessReasonTooLarge
83			}
84			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed
85		case ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired:
86			if out.Reason == "" {
87				out.Reason = ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope
88			}
89			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired
90		case ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine:
91			// Without a validated rewrite, a refinement need is not executable.
92			// The caller may override this to ActionableButRewritten after
93			// validating replacement text from the readiness payload.
94			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired
95		default:
96			out.Classification = ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
97			out.Reason = ReadinessReasonSystemUnready
98			out.SystemReason = ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable
99			out.TriageClassification = triageClassificationForSystemReason(out.SystemReason)
100			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeError
101		}
102		return out
103	}
104	
105	func normalizeReadinessClassification(classification string) string {
106		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(classification)) {
107		case "", "ready", "actionable_atomic", "atomic", "ok", "actionable", "pass":
108			return ReadinessClassificationReady
109		case "needs_refine", "actionable_but_rewritten", "rewritten":
110			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine
111		case "needs_split", "too_large", "too_large_decomposed", "decomposable":
112			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit
113		case "operator_required", "human_review_required":
114			return ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired
115		case "system_unready", "readiness_error", "intake_error":
116			return ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
117		default:
118			return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(classification))
119		}
120	}
121	
122	func normalizeReadinessReason(reason string) string {
123		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(reason)) {
124		case ReadinessReasonTooLarge,
125			ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope,
126			ReadinessReasonMissingRootCauseOrCurrentState,
127			ReadinessReasonMissingVerification,
128			ReadinessReasonMissingCodePathAssertion,
129			ReadinessReasonMissingDependencyOrParent,
130			ReadinessReasonHiddenExternalBlocker,
131			ReadinessReasonAlreadySatisfiedCandidate,
132			ReadinessReasonSystemUnready:
133			return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(reason))
134		default:
135			return strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(reason))
136		}
137	}
138	
139	func firstReadinessReason(reasons []string) string {
140		for _, reason := range reasons {
141			reason = normalizeReadinessReason(reason)
142			if reason != "" {
143				return reason
144			}
145		}
146		return ""
147	}
148	
149	func readinessClassificationForReason(reason string) string {
150		switch normalizeReadinessReason(reason) {
151		case ReadinessReasonTooLarge:
152			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit
153		case ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope, ReadinessReasonHiddenExternalBlocker:
154			return ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired
155		case ReadinessReasonMissingRootCauseOrCurrentState,
156			ReadinessReasonMissingVerification,
157			ReadinessReasonMissingCodePathAssertion,
158			ReadinessReasonMissingDependencyOrParent,
159			ReadinessReasonAlreadySatisfiedCandidate:
160			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine
161		case ReadinessReasonSystemUnready:
162			return ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
163		default:
164			return ""
165		}
166	}
167	
168	func classifyReadinessSystemReason(detail string, reasons []string) string {
169		combined := strings.ToLower(strings.Join(append([]string{detail}, reasons...), "\n"))
170		combined = strings.TrimSpace(combined)
171		if combined == "" {
172			return ""
173		}
174		switch {
175		case IsLockContentionError(combined):
176			return ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency
177		case containsAny(combined,
178			"resource_exhausted",
179			"resource exhausted",
180			"enospc",
181			"no space left on device",
182			"out of space",
183			"disk quota exceeded",
184			"temp root is full",
185			"cannot allocate memory"):
186			return ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted
187		case containsAny(combined, "worktree") && containsAny(combined,
188			"failed",
189			"lost",
190			"missing",
191			"cannot",
192			"no such file",
193			"not found",
194			"no space"):
195			return ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted
196		case containsAny(combined, "evidence") && containsAny(combined,
197			"write",
198			"persist",
199			"commit",
200			"bundle") && containsAny(combined,
201			"failed",
202			"no space",
203			"permission denied",
204			"i/o error"):
205			return ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted
206		case containsAny(combined,
207			"429",
208			"too many requests",
209			"rate limit",
210			"rate_limit",
211			"quota",
212			"usage ceiling",
213			"spend cap"):
214			return ReadinessSystemReasonQuota
215		case isTransportError(errors.New(combined)):
216			return ReadinessSystemReasonTransport
217		case containsAny(combined,
218			"resolveroute:",
219			"no viable routing candidate",
220			"no viable provider",
221			"no live provider",
222			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
223			"no harness configured",
224			"missing harness",
225			"harness not installed",
226			"unknown harness",
227			"executable file not found"):
228			return ReadinessSystemReasonRouting
229		default:
230			return ""
231		}
232	}
233	
234	func triageClassificationForSystemReason(systemReason string) string {
235		switch systemReason {
236		case ReadinessSystemReasonRouting:
237			return "routing"
238		case ReadinessSystemReasonQuota:
239			return "quota"
240		case ReadinessSystemReasonTransport:
241			return "transport"
242		case ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
243			ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency,
244			ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable:
245			return "recoverable"
246		default:
247			return ""
248		}
249	}
250	
251	func isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(reason string) bool {
252		switch reason {
253		case "routing",
254			"quota",
255			"transport",
256			"recoverable",
257			ReadinessSystemReasonResourceExhausted,
258			ReadinessSystemReasonRepoConcurrency,
259			FailureModeNoViableProvider,
260			FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled,
261			FailureModeLockContention,
262			FailureModeWorktreeLost:
263			return true
264		default:
265			return false
266		}
267	}
268

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	)
10	
11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
91		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
92		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
93			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
94		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
95		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98	
99		// Register "ddx work plan" and "ddx work focus" as subcommands of "ddx work".
100		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
102	
103		return cmd
104	}
105	
106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"io"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"regexp"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/executeloop"
16		agenttry "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/try"
17		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/work"
18		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
19		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
20		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
21		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
22	)
23	
24	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
25	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
26	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
27	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
28	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
29		Log             io.Writer
30		CleanupLog      io.Writer
31		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
32		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
33		CleanupInterval time.Duration
34		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
35		EventSink       io.Writer
36		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
37		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
38		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
39		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
40		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
41		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
42		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
43		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
44		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
45		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
46		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
47		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
48		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
49		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
50		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
51		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
52		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
53		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
54		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
55		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
56		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
57		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
58		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
59		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
60		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is

97	// NewPreClaimIntakeHook constructs the bead-intake complexity gate used
98	// before claim in the work / execute-loop paths. The hook evaluates the bead
99	// using the repository's triage prompt and returns one of the typed intake
100	// outcomes so the loop can decide whether to claim or skip the candidate.
101	//
102	// The hook uses the normal service execution path but clears hidden
103	// profile/power bounds so auxiliary readiness calls do not create DDx-side
104	// routing pins. Route failures are infrastructure failures, not bead-readiness
105	// decisions, so they return intake_error and let the loop use its fail-open
106	// readiness path.
107	func NewPreClaimIntakeHook(projectRoot string, store BeadReader, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner) func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
108		return func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
109			if ctx != nil {
110				if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
111					return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, err
112				}
113			}
114			if strings.TrimSpace(projectRoot) == "" {
115				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("pre-claim intake: project root required")
116			}
117			if err := ensureBeadLifecycleSkill(projectRoot); err != nil {
118				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("pre-claim intake: %w", err)
119			}
120			if store == nil {
121				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("pre-claim intake: bead reader required")
122			}
123	
124			b, err := store.Get(beadID)
125			if err != nil {
126				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("pre-claim intake: load bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
127			}
128	
129			prompt, err := buildPreClaimIntakePrompt(projectRoot, store, b)
130			if err != nil {
131				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("pre-claim intake: build prompt: %w", err)
132			}
133	
134			runtime := AgentRunRuntime{
135				Prompt:           prompt,
136				WorkDir:          projectRoot,
137				PromptSource:     PreClaimIntakePromptSource,
138				ProfileOverride:  selectProfileForDispatch(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, SelectStrongestProfile),
139				ClearRoutingPins: true,
140				ClearProfile:     true,
141				ClearMinPower:    true,
142				ClearMaxPower:    true,
143			}
144			payload, err := dispatchPreClaimIntakePayload(ctx, projectRoot, svc, runner, rcfg, runtime)
145			if err != nil {
146				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
147					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeError,
148					Detail:  preClaimIntakeRouteUnavailableDetail(err),
149				}, nil
150			}

1	// Package executeloop defines the canonical spec type for execute-loop workers.
2	// It is import-safe: no dependency on cobra, server, or transport packages.

12	// SpecCurrentVersion is the single spec version supported by this client and
13	// server. Forward-compat stance: a client MUST set SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion;
14	// a server receiving an unknown version SHOULD reject with an error rather than
15	// silently misinterpreting fields. There is no backwards-compat shim — upgrade
16	// client and server together.
17	const SpecCurrentVersion = 1
18	
19	// Mode controls how the execute-loop terminates.
20	type Mode string
21	
22	const (
23		// ModeOnce runs one bead (or none if the queue is empty) then exits.
24		ModeOnce Mode = "once"
25		// ModeDrain processes all ready beads then exits.
26		ModeDrain Mode = "drain"
27		// ModeWatch processes all ready beads, then sleeps for IdleInterval and
28		// rechecks, looping indefinitely until interrupted.
29		ModeWatch Mode = "watch"
30	)

32	// Duration wraps time.Duration with JSON support that marshals as a string
33	// (e.g., "30s") and unmarshals both strings ("30s") and numeric nanoseconds.
34	type Duration struct {
35		time.Duration
36	}
37	
38	func (d Duration) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
39		return json.Marshal(d.String())
40	}
41	
42	func (d *Duration) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
43		// Try string first.
44		var s string
45		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err == nil {
46			parsed, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
47			if err != nil {
48				return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: invalid duration string %q: %w", s, err)
49			}
50			d.Duration = parsed
51			return nil
52		}
53		// Try numeric nanoseconds.
54		var ns int64
55		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &ns); err == nil {
56			d.Duration = time.Duration(ns)
57			return nil
58		}
59		return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: cannot unmarshal %s as duration (want string or nanosecond integer)", strconv.Quote(string(b)))
60	}

115	// ApplyDefaults fills in zero-value fields with their canonical defaults.
116	// Safe to call on a zero-value spec.
117	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) ApplyDefaults() {
118		if s.Mode == "" {
119			s.Mode = ModeDrain
120		}
121		if s.Mode == ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration == 0 {
122			s.IdleInterval = Duration{30 * time.Second}
123		}
124		if s.SpecVersion == 0 {
125			s.SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion
126		}
127	}
128	
129	// Validate checks that the spec is well-formed after ApplyDefaults has run.
130	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) Validate() error {
131		switch s.Mode {
132		case ModeOnce, ModeDrain, ModeWatch:
133		default:
134			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unknown mode %q (want once, drain, or watch)", s.Mode)
135		}
136		if s.Mode != ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration != 0 {
137			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: idle_interval is only valid when mode=watch")
138		}
139		if s.SpecVersion != 0 && s.SpecVersion != SpecCurrentVersion {
140			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unsupported spec_version %d (want %d)", s.SpecVersion, SpecCurrentVersion)
141		}
142		return nil
143	}

- Key test: `TestServerWorker_WiresPreClaimIntakeHook` verifies server-managed workers invoke intake and lint hooks with correct sequencing

**3. `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-30bc30ed-20260510T005502-b32ad239/cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go`**
- Covers ddx-b790449b AC1/AC2 requirements
- Key test: `TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified` verifies:
  - Project with `.ddx/beads.jsonl` but no `.ddx/config.yaml` doesn't emit "routing under-specified" error
  - Drains against global agent config defaults instead
  - Does not create `.ddx/config.yaml` as a side effect

### Source Files Content

**4. `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-30bc30ed-20260510T005502-b32ad239/cli/cmd/work.go`**
- Implements `ddx work` command (FEAT-010 layer-3 queue drain)
- Treats `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model` as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged
- DDx does not validate or branch on constraint values
- Owns retry-power policy between attempts (min-power/max-power)
- Review is on by default; `--no-review` requires `--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing` acknowledgement
- Stop conditions: drained, blocked, deferred, no_progress, signal
- Runs inline in current process (per ADR-022; no separate "submit to server" mode)

**5. `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-30bc30ed-20260510T005502-b32ad239/cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go`**
- Implements deterministic bridge between bead-lifecycle readiness vocabulary and execute-loop scheduling semantics
- Core function: `ClassifyReadiness()` maps evidence to `ReadinessClassificationResult`
- Infrastructure signals always win over bead-quality signals (routing, quota, transport, resource, repo-concurrency failures don't park as needs_refine/needs_split)
- Readiness classifications: ready, needs_refine, needs_split, operator_required, system_unready
- Readiness reasons: too_large, ambiguous_scope, missing_root_cause, missing_verification, missing_code_path_assertion, missing_dependency, hidden_external_blocker, already_satisfied_candidate, system_unready
- System reasons: unavailable, routing, quota, transport, resource_exhausted, repo_concurrency
- PreClaimIntakeOutcome mapping connects to outcomes: actionable_atomic, too_large_decomposed, operator_required, error

**6. `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-30bc30ed-20260510T005502-b32ad239/cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go`**
- Tests readiness classification logic
- Key tests:
  - `TestReadinessClassification_DoesNotMisclassifyInfrastructureAsBeadDefect`: Ensures infrastructure issues (missing_harness, provider_quota, transport, enospc, worktree_setup, evidence_write, git_index_lock) are correctly classified as system_unready
  - `TestReadinessClassification_BeadDefectsUseReadinessReasons`: Verifies bead quality issues map to correct classifications
  - `TestReadinessClassification_DecodesReadinessSchema`: Tests decoding of payload results
  - `TestReadinessClassification_DeterministicSystemReasonBypassesModelTriage`: Ensures deterministic system reasons aren't overwritten by model triage

All files are in Go, following a clear separation between test verification (test files) and implementation (source files).

2	ddx:
3	  id: TD-031
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-004
6	    - SD-004
7	    - ADR-004
8	---
9	# Technical Design: Bead State Machine and Naming-Role Decisions
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	This TD is the canonical reference for what bead state means in DDx and where
14	each observed name in the codebase belongs. It exists because schema, docs,
15	code, and persisted data have drifted: the bead-record JSON Schema enumerates
16	six statuses (the bd/br canonical set), FEAT-004 still documents three, and
17	several legacy/backcompat names appear in code paths (`done`, `needs_human`,
18	`pending`, `ready`, `review`, `needs_investigation`) without a single decision
19	record explaining whether each is a status, a derived queue category, an
20	event kind, a label, or worker state.
21	
22	The five in-flight hygiene beads each introduce new state-machine vocabulary:
23	
24	- ddx-b24e9630 — `no_changes_*` outcome verification (NoChangesContract)
25	- ddx-3c154349 — auto-triage of stuck beads (TriageContract)
26	- ddx-aede917d — drain pause on quota exhaustion (QuotaPauseContract)
27	- ddx-c6e3db02 — rate-limit retry behavior (RateLimitRetryContract)
28	- ddx-da11a34a — main-git/tracker lock contention handling
29	  (LockContentionContract)
30	
31	Without one TD that nails down the categories and the transition matrix,
32	those five beads will each independently invent overlapping vocabulary.
33	
34	This TD authors the design only. The mechanical reconciliation
35	(schema-vs-FEAT-004 alignment, code rename of non-status names, CI guards,
36	hygiene-bead AC substitution, migration survey) is filed as sibling beads.
37	
38	## Critical Constraint (per ADR-004)
39	
40	The bead-record envelope must remain compatible with the bd/br interchange
41	contract. The persisted `status` enum is **fixed** at the bd/br canonical six
42	values:
43	
44	```
45	open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled
46	```
47	
48	DDx-specific execution semantics live in **labels**, **events**, or the
49	preserved-extras **Extra** map — never in new statuses. Adding a new persisted
50	status requires upstream bd/br coordination plus an ADR-004 amendment. This
51	TD does not authorize any such addition.
52	
53	## Today's Observed State (verified)
54	
55	- `cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json` enumerates six values.
56	- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md` line 65 still documents
57	  three values (`open, in_progress, closed`). FEAT-004 lags the schema.
58	- `ddx bead list --json | jq -r '.[].status' | sort -u` on real projects
59	  returns only `open`, `in_progress`, `closed` for active rows today —
60	  `blocked`, `proposed`, and `cancelled` are schema-allowed but unused in
61	  the current active queue.
62	- The legacy/backcompat names `done`, `needs_human`, `pending`, `ready`, `review`,
63	  `needs_investigation` appear in code paths but are **not** persisted bead
64	  statuses today. Each falls into one of the other categories defined below.
65	
66	## Sections
67	
68	The remainder of this TD is structured as the eleven design sections required
69	by the originating bead, followed by five per-hygiene-bead contract
70	subsections.
71	
72	---
73	
74	## 1. Category Taxonomy
75	
76	Every state-machine name observed in DDx falls into exactly one of the
77	following categories. The category determines where the name is allowed to
78	live (schema, label namespace, event kind, queue derivation, or worker
79	process state).
80	
81	| Category | Storage location | Lifecycle | Owner |
82	|---|---|---|---|
83	| Persisted bead status | `status` field on the bead record | Mutated by atomic snapshot rewrite | Bead store, locked to bd/br set |
84	| Derived queue category | Computed on read from status + deps + preserved metadata | Never persisted | Queue derivation code (`ddx bead ready/blocked/status`) |
85	| Event kind | Append-only entry in `Extra["events"][].kind` | Append-only; explains state, does not control lifecycle | Drain loop, agent service, CLI |
86	| Terminal phase | A persisted `closed` status plus a closing event/label that names *why* | Mutated once on close | Drain loop / CLI |
87	| Claim metadata | `assignee`, `claimed-at`, `claimed-pid` fields (preserved extras) | Set on claim, cleared on unclaim, expired by triage | Claim resolution path (TD-004) |
88	| Label | Entry in the `labels` array | Explains or filters state; never controls lifecycle | Anyone with `ddx bead update` |
89	| Extra metadata field | Arbitrary key under preserved extras | Explains or filters state; never controls lifecycle | Subsystem owning that key |
90	| Worker state | In-memory state of the drain process | Lives only for the worker's lifetime | Drain loop process |
91	
92	A name MUST NOT span categories. If a name today appears as both a label and
93	a derived queue category (e.g., `ready` is derived; `ready` MUST NOT also
94	appear as a label), the sibling reconciliation bead removes the duplicate
95	usage.
96	
97	## 2. Persisted Status Enumeration
98	
99	The persisted bead status is exactly:
100	
101	```
102	open | in_progress | closed | blocked | proposed | cancelled
103	```
104	
105	This is the bd/br-canonical set; it matches `bead-record.schema.json`. This
106	TD does **not** add, rename, or remove any persisted status.
107	
108	Plain-English semantics:
109	
110	- `open` — accepted active work. No claim is active. This includes work that is
111	  waiting on dependencies; dependency waiting is derived from the dependency DAG
112	  while the persisted status remains `open`.
113	- `in_progress` — the bead has an active claim (`assignee`, `claimed-at`,
114	  `claimed-pid` populated). The drain loop or an operator has taken
115	  ownership.
116	- `closed` — terminal satisfied work. A `closed` dependency satisfies
117	  downstream beads. The reason for closure is encoded in a closing event and/or
118	  label; the status itself does not carry the reason.
119	- `blocked` — accepted work paused by a rare external, recheckable blocker.
120	  Dependency waits are not `status=blocked`; they are derived from unsatisfied
121	  dependencies while the bead remains `open`.
122	- `proposed` — operator decision required. Proposed beads are not
123	  autonomous-work eligible until an operator accepts, rewrites, splits, waives,
124	  or cancels them.
125	- `cancelled` — terminal not-doing. Distinct from `closed`: `cancelled` does
126	  not satisfy dependents unless a later explicit dependency policy says
127	  otherwise.
128	
129	## 3. Transition Matrix
130	
131	| From → To | Allowed? | Driver | Event fired |
132	|---|---|---|---|
133	| `proposed` → `open` | yes | operator (`ddx bead update --status open`) | `triaged` |
134	| `proposed` → `cancelled` | yes | operator | `cancelled` |
135	| `open` → `proposed` | yes | readiness or operator found missing decision input | `triage-ambiguous` / `review-manual-required` |
136	| `open` → `in_progress` | yes | drain loop or operator (claim) | `claimed` |
137	| `open` → `blocked` | yes | operator or auto-triage found an external recheckable blocker | `blocked` |
138	| `open` → `cancelled` | yes | operator | `cancelled` |
139	| `in_progress` → `open` | yes | unclaim (operator or stale-claim sweep) | `unclaimed` |
140	| `in_progress` → `closed` | yes | drain loop (on merge/already-satisfied) or operator | `closed-merged` / `closed-already-satisfied` |
141	| `in_progress` → `proposed` | yes | non-automatable review/readiness finding or exhausted repair/review budget | `review-block` / `review-manual-required` |
142	| `in_progress` → `blocked` | yes | drain loop or operator found an external recheckable blocker | `blocked` |
143	| `blocked` → `open` | yes | operator (block resolved) | `unblocked` |
144	| `blocked` → `cancelled` | yes | operator | `cancelled` |
145	| `closed` → * | no | — | — (closed is terminal) |
146	| `cancelled` → * | no | — | — (cancelled is terminal) |
147	
148	Closed and cancelled are terminal. `closed` satisfies dependency edges;
149	`cancelled` does not satisfy dependency edges unless a future dependency policy
150	explicitly defines an exception. Re-opening a closed bead is not a transition;
